review_text:
Went there to visit Bienale Arte di Venezia.
There is also a caffeteria where you can eat a delicious sandwich and a great cup of Italian coffee while visiting the pavillions.
Pics are from 2024.

The bienalle for architecture is both mind blowing and overwhelming. The 2025 installation was very focused on climate change, global growth and usage of resources and how materials/technologies are used for global problem solving. It is important to go in with an open mind and time to process the information. Some countries and exhibits are better than others in terms of interpretation and information. For the year 2025, the beauty is in the details. The exhibition u Is not about architecture as many think of it, but rather about elements of architecture. And, if you are not comfortable with the English or Italian language, you will likely be lost and confused. It is wise to get the three-day pass and break up your time to allow the ability to process the density of information. There are many restaurants and cafes in the Giardini but in the main bienalle exhibit in the arsenale area there is only one large cafe and restaurant. Be sure to download area maps before you explore to get a good sense of place and understand the exhibits before you explore. The water taxis will bring you to the main stops for arsenale and giardini.

Compared to the rest of Venice, I found Giardini to be incredibly peaceful. Unlike much of the city, it's lush with greenery—trees, flowers, and open spaces. There are plenty of benches, and I enjoyed watching birds and butterflies flutter around. I came for the Biennale, but it was the tranquility of Giardini that truly impressed me.

Beautiful location for the biennial art and architecture Biennales. At the Giardini are most of the national pavilions. At the 2025 Architecture Biennale I was particularly impressed by the US and Scandinavian pavilions with the former focusing on the porch in US architecture with great examples like Ocean Grove, New Jersey and Chautauqua, New York. The Greek pavilion was impressive as well with a focus on the two Venetian shipyards on the island of Crete. The Israeli pavilion was closed for renovation as was the pavilion of Venezuela, designed by Carlo Scarpa (1906-1978) who also designed the original entrance pavilion to the Biennale.

Amid the bustling canals and throngs of visitors that define Venice, the Giardini della Biennale offers a breath of calm. This leafy enclave, tucked along the Bacino di San Marco, isn’t a destination for grand botanical wonders, but its quiet charm and historical roots make it a worthy detour for those seeking respite.

Originally marshland transformed by Napoleon into public gardens, the space now serves dual purposes: a peaceful retreat and a cultural hub. Its winding paths lead visitors past 30 pavilions, each representing a different nation and showcasing architectural flair from icons like Carlo Scarpa and Alvar Aalto. Though the structures truly come alive during the Venice Biennale Art Festival—when global creativity takes center stage—they retain a quiet magnetism year-round.

The gardens’ unpretentious beauty is amplified by quirky details: feral cats lounging in sunlit corners, the stoic gaze of Garibaldi’s statue at the entrance, and a serene coastal walk that skirts the water’s edge. These elements stitch together a patchwork of tranquility, a stark contrast to the nearby chaos of St. Mark’s Square.

Yet, for all its understated appeal, the Giardini della Biennale remains a “nice-to-have” rather than a must-see. If your Venice itinerary is packed, you won’t miss a life-changing experience by skipping it. But for those with moments to spare, it’s a gentle reminder that even in a city overrun by tourism, pockets of stillness—and a few curious cats—still endure.

Stumbled upon Giardini Reali just behind Piazza San Marco, and it instantly became my go-to oasis in the city. With shaded benches, pergola-covered walkways, and a lovely view of the lagoon, it’s the perfect spot to recharge between sightseeing bursts  .

Unexpected calm: Even on busy days, the garden’s tucked-away location makes it feel almost secret—perfect for a pause with a book or a snack  .

Lovely design touches: A restored 19th-century greenhouse, drawbridge to Piazza San Marco, and the longest wisteria pergola in Venice give it both charm and elegance  .

Free and easy access: Open to the public, and ideal for hanging out while you wait for the vaporetto at Giardinetti stop. No tickets, no rush  .

review_text:
Tucked away just around the corner from the bustling St. Mark’s Square, the Gardens of the Venice Foundation (Giardini della Fondazione Venezia) are a true hidden gem—a quiet haven of greenery and tranquility in a city better known for its canals and narrow alleys. We stumbled upon this secret spot during our visit and ended up spending a wonderfully peaceful hour here, simply relaxing with an ice cream and enjoying the natural beauty.

The gardens have an interesting history, originally part of the private grounds of a noble Venetian palace. Today, they are one of the few green spaces in the historic center, offering a rare escape from the crowds. The well-maintained paths wind through a delightful mix of Mediterranean plants, shady trees, and blooming flowers, creating an atmosphere that feels worlds away from the nearby tourist hotspots.

One of the most enjoyable aspects was the sense of seclusion—even though we were only minutes from the heart of Venice, the gardens felt almost undiscovered. The benches and quiet corners make it an ideal spot to pause, unwind, and soak in the beauty of the surroundings.

For anyone looking for a moment of calm in the midst of Venice’s energy, this is the perfect place. Whether you’re escaping the midday heat, looking for a quiet place to read, or simply wanting to appreciate another side of the city, the Gardens of the Venice Foundation are well worth seeking out.

The gardens were created in 1807 by order of Napoleon Bonaparte and designed by Gianantonio Selva as a public green space for the citizens of Venice. Today, they serve both as a peaceful retreat and as a major cultural venue. Since 1895, the gardens have hosted the Venice Biennale, beginning with its first International Art Exhibition. During the Biennale, approximately 65% of the gardens are dedicated to exhibition space, while the remaining 35% remains open as public parkland. Starting in 1907, individual nations began constructing their own pavilions within the gardens. Today, there are 29 national pavilions, making the gardens one of the central hubs for contemporary art during the Biennale.

Surprising oasis of peace in a hectic city.  Come here, sit, walk your dog and enjoy the quiet.
Parco di Villa Groggia is a serene, lesser-known park in Venice's Cannaregio district, offering a peaceful retreat from the city's bustle. Visitors can enjoy shaded paths, benches, and the charming Teatrino Groggia, a small 19th-century theater hosting community events .

Historically, the park occupies the site of the 16th-century Palazzo Donà, demolished in 1823. In the late 19th century, Giuseppe Groggia developed the area into a romantic-style garden. After his death, the property was bequeathed to the San Gioacchino Institute in Mestre for the education of underprivileged girls. Since 1975, the City of Venice has owned and maintained the park .

Today, Parco di Villa Groggia serves as a tranquil space for locals and visitors alike, featuring shaded areas, benches, and a community center, making it an ideal spot to relax and experience a quieter side of Venice.

We were hoping to find a park for the kids, but were disappointed that there is really nothing there… Although it is a pretty spot.
Frumoas colt de rai!
The park is closed to the public. But the street is really quite and looks over one of the many canals
Un angolo bellissimo ma è aperto al pubblico?

shop_address: Via Giuseppe Garibaldi, 1254, 30122 loc. Castello VE, Italy
review_text: this is just an incredible place!! I would like to express my deepest gratitude to my friend Vera for planning a walk to this cafe for us❣️This is how I imagined my dream cafe.. what windows there are!! My friends and I had a great time there, some of us even managed to take a nap. Also, appreciate the sound of the cicadas, you can hear them in the video.

shop_address: Via Giuseppe Garibaldi, 1254, 30122 loc. Castello VE, Italy
review_text: I’ve recently came across this spectacular cafe in Venice. It sure has a very different vibe and atmosphere from all the other bars in Venice. It’s located in a small beautiful garden and they also sell plants. The plants, high roof, and the glass windows make it a very pretty place for relaxing and enjoying your day. We tried the hot chocolate with cream which I really liked, and the sweets (cupcake and small cornetto) tasted good too. The prices are about 1€ higher that other bars in Venice.

shop_address: Via Giuseppe Garibaldi, 1254, 30122 loc. Castello VE, Italy
review_text:
Nice architecture.
Stuff don’t speak English.
No reservations needed.
Mini brioche 1€, torta salata with spinaci 5€.  Small portions.
Overrated place, with high prices. Photogenic architecture and garden if you’re into Instagram places.
But if you want to have a nice breakfast and cappuccino better go somewhere else.
Hygiene not on a good level, interior full of dust. Nice industrial look that could use some cleaning.
Not recommended.

shop_address: Via Giuseppe Garibaldi, 1254, 30122 loc. Castello VE, Italy
review_text: Staff took forever to take our orders. The bathroom is particularly dirty unfortunately and there should be more people taking care of the service table. Having only 1 waitress to take order AND pay the bills and only 1 waitress to clean the tables in a busy Sunday is not a good idea. The inside is really nice and the ambience is alright so the stars are all for that.

shop_address: Via Giuseppe Garibaldi, 1254, 30122 loc. Castello VE, Italy
review_text:
Visited this place on the 19th of June 2024. Decent prices, decent drinks. I've visited it twice on the same day, at lunchtime, trying to find some shade, and once later the afternoon while my spose was visiting the Giardini della Bienalle . The shade was not the best, I can't really say that umbrellas provide the best shade, but the drinks were awesome (had some americano coffees, a machiato, some sprits) . They also had a power outlet inside so i charged my device while sipping my drinks.

Plus points for the venue, a former greenhouse dating back to 1890s.
Minus points for not having AC and maybe the waiters were a bit slow but spoke English and that was great.

I will add screenshots with their menu and peices as of June 2024 downloaded with their QR.

My first time experience there was super ok. Perfetto, like the italians would say 😁

shop_address: Via Giuseppe Garibaldi, 1254, 30122 loc. Castello VE, Italy
review_text: The salad+Burrata Bowl was fresh, and amazing, the Staff-crew was also very kind and the place itself is just a hidden gem. Cozy, place to stay.. have a drink and just enjoy the sun. Thankyou for having us!

shop_address: Via Giuseppe Garibaldi, 1254, 30122 loc. Castello VE, Italy
review_text: Very nice place. However it is full of tourists and you need to wait for the table, even if you want just quick coffee.

shop_address: Via Giuseppe Garibaldi, 1254, 30122 loc. Castello VE, Italy
review_text:
A picturesque place, I loved that special calming atmosphere as you are sitting in the old greenhouse surrounded by plants.   The staff was nice. The café has a wide offer of drinks. We had a hot chocolate and it was delicious. What is more, they have loose tea, which is not common in Italy in my opinion.
Another bonus: they have a piano and guitar you can play!
I will definitely return there!

Ein wirklich entspannender Ort inmitten der quirligen Stadt Venedig. Hier ist ein botanischer Garten nach dem Buch der Heiligen Theresa angelegt worden. Das Consorzio Vini Venezia kultiviert in diesem außergewöhnlichen Garten, 18 verschiedene Rebsorten aus denen auch Wein produziert wird. Verantwortlich ist hier Pater Hermano.
Die Mönche stellen auch verschiedene Heilmittel aus Melissa her, die in der Klosterapotheke käuflich erworben werden können!
Tolle Produkte aus meist eigener Produktion!
Antichissima struttura. Ben tenuta. Luogo caratteristico
Mistico.

Great place in Murano to have some fresh air and to relax under the trees. There are tables, so you can bring your own food and take a seat. Also there's a small playground so children can have fun too.
Dogs are allowed in the area.
Nice quiet park with trees, benches and grass. Play structure with 2 slides and swings. Good place to take a break, eat lunch and let kids play.

This is really an oasis in Murano in order to let the kids enjoy a playground. Since there are few attractions designed for kids in Venice, it is quite challenging to entertain them.
So, this was a good place to enjoy a good time with tons of tranquility and fresh environment.
It should be better if there were a coffee shop, but like this, is already perfect.
great break for kids to burn some energy. lots of trees on an otherwise barren island.  easy to let kids play while an adult goes shopping
Great surprise in Murano
We have to beg my little brother before, we could brig him out from there. He was enjoying heself running up & down hahahahah
Good park for toddler break in Murano.
Okay park for kids to let of some steam.

shop_address: Campo Santa Margherita, 2919, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
I’ve visited Venice twice this year and eaten at the lots of restaurants, and this place is above the best so far!!  All dishes are cooked with perfection and they use really fresh product.
Service was correct and professional, they had great wine selection. Apero Spritz was great too.
Thanks for the fantastic experience!! We really had great time :)

shop_address: Campo Santa Margherita, 2919, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
Situated amongst a busy plaza square, this is a beautiful restaurant with good food. We ate here before continuing our walk to the jazz club for a show. We were one of the first ones to arrive at dinner opening and got a table outside on the patio right away. As time went on, the restaurant became very busy and every table was taken. It became clear that the server was a bit overwhelmed as he was the only one attending to all tables outside. Nonetheless, still a great experience and it’s always a good rule of thumb to not be rushed when dining in Italy.
For the food, my partner ordered scallops and I ordered bruschetta. The scallops were not the best we’ve ever had but still good. My biggest con was that the olive oil was served in a reusable oil container and the cleanliness of the container was questionable. There were discolored spots within the container/oil. I had never seen olive oil in Italy served outside of its standard bottle.
We then ordered the lasagna to share as an entree. Now, the lasagna was some of the best I have ever had. It had an intense sweet note to it which was different and neat. My partner ordered some chocolate with an inner cookie type filling dessert and that did not last longer than 4 mins on the plate. Overall, a good experience and is a lovely place to eat outside on the patio and people watch with good food.

shop_address: Campo Santa Margherita, 2919, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: My sister and I came for a lovely meal. I had gluten free spaghetti carbonara and it was delicious! My sister loved the ravioli. Marco was an amazing waiter. We had great service. Could not recommend more ! :) 10/10

shop_address: Campo Santa Margherita, 2919, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Loved our waiter, full of good humor and swift service. It was enjoyable to be served professionally by a local that joyfully called out to locals as they passed by. Everything was fresh and homemade… I love

shop_address: Campo Santa Margherita, 2919, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
I’m a frequent traveler and restaurant-goer, but I rarely leave reviews—unless I’m truly impressed. And this restaurant completely blew me away. Everything was perfect: the location, the quality of the food, and the service!

I came two days in a row, and both times the experience was fantastic. I’m sharing photos of the three courses I ordered—because pictures speak louder than words. Each course was a masterpiece. Compliments to the Chef!

The atmosphere on Campo Santa Margherita is another great bonus—it's one of the most spacious squares in Venice and not overrun by tourist crowds.

Finally, I want to highlight Sali, our waiter, who is a true artist at what he does! I later found out that this is a family-run restaurant and that he’s actually a co-owner. That explained why I felt so welcome—the incredible quality here is clearly driven by love and dedication.

All in all, based on my many dining experiences around the world, this is easily one of the best restaurants I’ve visited in my 57 years. Highly recommended!

shop_address: Campo Santa Margherita, 2919, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: We had such a great evening here! The restaurant is small and cozy, with a few tables outside, and it’s decorated really nicely. The food was absolutely delicious and the service made the whole experience even better. Huge thanks to Marco and his brother-in-law – amazing people who made our night extra special. Grazie!!! ♥️

shop_address: Campo Santa Margherita, 2919, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Me and my partner had a stunning meal here, the waiters were attentive, the food was to die for, and the atmosphere was lovely with a great setting on a popular but not intrusive square. Would highly recommend

shop_address: Campo Santa Margherita, 2919, 30123 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: The raw seafood selection was okay. Some of the items were delicious but the oysters were the saltiest I’ve ever had, however the scallop was very good. The ravioli was good but I don’t think it’d be worth a visit back. They had a nice cat hanging out inside the restaurant.

shop_name: Giardino di Palazzo Corner della Ca' Granda
shop_address: C. del Dose da Ponte, 30100 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Giardino di Palazzo Corner della Ca'Granda shows currently the „peace crystal“ of artist Mariko Mori.
Perfect place to chill and read after a little walk!
Not crowded place, there are a few tourists.
An area for the intrepid who wants to experience Venice in less crowded quarters.
I spend in this hotel 2 nights. Very good for short time. Close to city centre, clean. Breakfast nothing special
The Last island in Venice, seat of the Venice Cathedral (not to be confused with St.Mark's Basilica).
You may get a whole different sensation of Venice if you come up to here.
Quiet and peaceful. Relax away from the crowds.
A wonderful experience

shop_address: Corte dei Santi, 30100 Scolastica, VE, Italy
review_text: Pałac Dożów Wenecja coś pięknego trzeba to zobaczyć

This was a beautiful experience. There is a small tour which gives you enough information to inform and make the experience meaningful, but the tour guide allows you to experience the walk and music with yourself. The music composed specifically for the walk through the labyrinth is gorgeous, and walking quietly through while listening is a meditative experience, even if you are not a Borges readers. It was lightly raining, with sun, while we were there which added to the beauty!

We did this as a paired tour with the Vatican Chapels, and together they were an excellent experience.

We would love to experience it in another season, and will probably go back! Thank you!

Know before you go:
-You must book a ticket in advance through the Cini Foundation website.
-This is a symbolic labyrinth, not meant to be like a tourist corn maze or the like, more akin to a meditation garden.
-You should bring your own headphones if you can.

The maze is 1km to walk thru. There is a wonderful story behind the maze that the tour explains.
Booking the tour is the only way to walk through the maze also

Fabulous and mostly undiscovered addition to Venice.  Quick trip from San Zachariah across the bay to the Island.  Reservation for the Labyrinth required (with good reason - too many people would spoil the magic).  No reservation needed for the bell tower, but do bring cash for the elevator ticket.  I think it was 10 euro.
Fantastic place and well deserved homage to the greatest Latin American writer.
Fun experience, get just enough explanation of the labyrinth from the guided tour and musical accompaniment as you walk through. Requires pre-booking

Terrible exeperience! Came
with two children to only visit the labyrinth and it was already fully booked. If you want to visit, book the visit earlier (1-2 days prior)!!
You're not able to visit the labyrinth by yourself, only with a guided tour during certain hours of the day, which you have to book in advance.
The labyrinth is nice but if you want to see and visit it you have to pay 14 euro: too much for a audio registered audio guide. Not worth the money.

The room was uncomfortably hot and when we sent a message via WhatsApp we never got a response for the AC unit. There were also bugs in the room. The lack of communication was frustrating. Shared bathroom and common space were overall cleanly besides other people’s hairs in the bathroom sink and floor. There isn’t reinforcement of quiet hours and thin walls/floors as we woke up numerous times between 12-3am from the people up above us. Best thing about this stay is the location.
Everything matches the description, cousy room, great place, near is water bus station. Clean common bathroom and kitchen.

Excellent location. Not far from the train station and the old Jewish Ghetto.
Boat stop to Morano right outside.
Sharing a bathroom with 4 other rooms was the only downside.

出國那麼多地方，從來沒有住過那麼糟糕的房間（我是住單人房）。
第一，不僅要過好好幾關像廢墟的通道，才能進到房間，有夠麻煩，中途還有假人嚇你。
其次，一進房間讓我全身起雞皮疙瘩和不舒服。房間小就算了，有股難聞的味道，噴很多香水都無法蓋過，整個感到噁心，完全不想多待。
第三，房間已經夠小了，還放很大台又吵的空調，但不開又超悶，崩潰。
第四，總共四個房間卻要共要一個衛浴，實在不合理。
第一次不舒服到直接花錢訂另外一間，不然真的是花錢受罪。
最後，可以理解威尼斯本島的物價會比較貴和房偏小。但這間網路照片與現實不符，感覺被詐騙，大家不要來這邊住。

Delizioso piccolo albergo in una posizione vincente. Alcune cose andrebbero cambiate ma in linea di massima il soggiorno è stato positivo anche per la posizione su Cannaregio.|Letto comodo ed aria condizionata molto efficiente.|Gli ingressi sono piuttosto bui e ad accogliere l'ospite c'è una simpatica maschera veneziana.

No tuve ningún problema con el alojamiento. Todo limpio y pieza adecuada. El baño es compartido pero no tuve ningún problema. Es algo antiguo, pero esperable para una ciudad como Venecia.
Todo es caminable desde el alojamiento :)

A calm and restful green space in this area. It is well shaded with trees, with grassy areas for sitting or playing. There are plenty of benches to sit on for rest. On the edge of this Campo there is a very good Osteria, and a small grocery and bread shop, and a butcher shop that makes sandwiches. Nearby is an excellent fruit and vegetable shop. It is quiet here and somewhere the residents of this place come out to in the evening.
Calm and peaceful area in Venice!

Esp
Es precioso este parquesito, la luz en la tarde lo hace bien  bonito para estar sentados un gran rato, para mi mala suerte por la epoca que vine no habia mucha vida, vi que esta la osteria y por las fotos que subieron se nota que tiene mucha vida la zona en otra epoca del año.
It
Questo parchetto è bellissimo, la luce del pomeriggio rende molto piacevole stare seduti a lungo, per mia sfortuna perché la volta che sono venuto non c'era molta vita, ho visto che l'osteria c'è e dalle foto si caricato mostra che ha molta vita nella zona in un altro periodo dell'anno.
Bel campo veneziano in una parte meno conosciuta della città.
Magico💕💕💕💕💕😁

shop_address: Viale IV Novembre, 48, 30175 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: We loved this property so much we extended our stay twice! The location is absolutely wonderful for a family, and/or anyone with pets. The large park and play area were a big hit for our youngest two boys, who spent several hours a day exploring at the park. The water bus just a few steps away was fantastic for getting around, but we also loved the walk along the water. It’s a great way to get in some exercise after eating all of the wonderful food Venice has to offer! Other things we loved about the property were the large, comfortable rooms with plenty of seating for everyone, the well-equipped kitchen, and a printer!! We do online schooling and I can’t even begin to tell you how wonderful it was to have a laser printer available for our use!! The WiFi was exceptional as well. with four kids doing schoolwork and two adults utilizing the WiFi for our digital office we never experienced drag or downtimes. Great stay!

shop_address: Viale IV Novembre, 48, 30175 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Late to review, we stayed at this place last October, Location is perfect from inner chaos, loved the house, very tidy and perfect house for family and group of friends. Very good parks outside and station is a minute walk.

shop_address: Viale IV Novembre, 48, 30175 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Super great place to stay!

shop_address: Viale IV Novembre, 48, 30175 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Ottima sistemazione. La casa si trova in una posizione ottimale perché immersa nel verde, vicinissima alla fermata del vaporetto  s'Elena, e divisa perfettamente con due camere da letto un divano letto e un altro letto singolo. La cucina perfettamente attrezzata ed il bagno completo. Inoltre ha uno spazio esterno con piante dove si può mangiare nel silenzio più assoluto. Il proprietario Franco è stato disponibilissimo. Consiglio assolutamente.

shop_address: Viale IV Novembre, 48, 30175 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
Alojamiento muy recomendable! En una zona perfecta para ir en familia.
Parques, zonas verdes…
Y solo a 20’ caminando del centro o 15’ en vapporeto.

Un 10! Muy recomendable

shop_address: Viale IV Novembre, 48, 30175 Venezia VE, Italy
Viale IV Novembre, 48, 30175 Venezia VE, Italy

review_text: I WOULD’VE said I’d stay here again. That is no longer true. ||My husband, myself, and our infant daughter stayed here three nights. The first day was great; they actually gave us a bigger room than booked so our daughter had room to move around. Gave us the wifi, asked if we wanted breakfast included, etc, standard hotel things. The AC was difficult to work and were exhausted so we just went without overnight. Our daughter was really hot and sweaty and fell asleep an hour later than usual once we put a cool washcloth on her. In the morning, we were finally able to figure out the AC and left the hotel to explore for the day, excited to come back to a comfortable room. ||When we got back to our room in the late afternoon, the AC had been shut off and it was hotter in our room than outside. It was annoying but whatever so we turned it back on. We got some snacks for the night as I’m breastfeeding and get hungry in the middle of the night and didn’t think about it again. ||Until the third day. Our daughter woke up super early and they have quiet hours so we slipped out, got some pastries, and slipped back in to our room to not disturb anyone. There was also a sign with an X through it on pictures of food on the front door, so I was trying to be cautious that it seemed they didn’t want food in the foyer, which had some nice furniture in it. ||We went out for the day and when we came back in the afternoon, our daughter was screaming crying and overly tired, so we were hurrying back. When we got into the lobby, we were stopped by a new worker who told us that we were not allowed to have food in our room. I was confused why they were telling us this when we had openly brought up pastries and gelato a night before to our room and had not been told this rule. They said they found crumbs of our pastries and that food was not allowed but could be eaten in the common area which was behind the foyer. I was flustered by this so just said okay, explained about our tired baby, and took our daughter upstairs for her nap. ||My husband put our key in the lock and turned it both ways but it wouldn’t budge. He went downstairs and explained and the same lady came up with a master key and unlocked it. A special lock had been put in place that couldn’t be opened by our key, only hers. We took that to mean they did that so we would have to come down and be told about the food rule. They quite literally locked us out of our own hotel room that we had hurried back to for our screaming daughter just to make sure they told us to not eat food in the room. And the cherry on top, we got into the room, and the AC was off, AGAIN, leaving the room hot and it took longer for our daughter to fall asleep, needing a wet washcloth again. ||We were not told the rule about the food when we first came in nor we were told not to use the AC. The AC thing just seems like a  way to save money but it made us miserable especially when we were sweating from outside. ||The food rule also just seems dumb. We were only on the first floor, but this rule seemed excessive for someone on a much higher floor. Our hotel room is also the only spot our daughter could move around freely, so the idea of leaving it to go downstairs and eat a quick snack knowing she would be squirming around was not an easy concept to come to. Furthermore, there’s a tea kettle and a fridge in the room. If food isn’t allowed in the room, why the fridge? And outside of the one sticker on the door leading into the hotel, there was no other notice about the rule, which meant it was wildly up for interpretation. I took it to mean no food in the lobby because I’ve never been to a hotel that forbid food in the room. A better system would have been signs in the room, but there were none. ||This seems to me like a new rule. I looked at previous reviews and it seems they had an unresolved ant problem from September 2025, just a month earlier, so I’m guessing this is their bandaged way to fix the issue without getting rid of the ants. ||The location is great. But locking a guest out of their room, turning their AC off several times, and telling someone they can’t eat in their room isn’t telling of a hotel, but made me feel like I was in a dormitory.

I stayed at Apostoli Gardens and overall had a good experience. The room was clean, comfortable, and felt safe, though a bit on the smaller side. Prices are slightly high, but the location justifies it. The staff were extremely polite and welcoming, which made the stay even better. The Wi-Fi worked well throughout my stay, but the air conditioning could have been stronger.

We are a couple from Russia and Finland, and we stayed here for 2 days. We were very pleased with our room! The room was clean, well-maintained, and had a kettle, microwave, hairdryer, cups, and tea and coffee. There was also a TV, but we didn't use it. Most importantly, the room had excellent air conditioning and Wi-Fi. The price was excellent, and the quality was high. The bed and pillows were comfortable and cozy. On the second day of our stay, the room was cleaned and all the bedding and towels were replaced. I was delighted! The hotel is in a great location, convenient for shopping. I am very grateful to the receptionist who worked from 8.07-10.07.25, I was too shy to ask her name, but she was so friendly and welcoming! Best wishes from us to the hotel and the staff!💐❤️
Lovely little hotel in great location for water buses to Murano and St Marks Sq. Room small but clean shower a challenge. Staff very friendly and helpful. Breakfast fresh and plentiful
We loved our stay here - great location and a clean room. They also let us check in early and leave our luggage for a day before we left Venice. Would highly recommend!

This is the second time staying at Hotel Apostoli Garden, I booked direct with the hotel, one Queen bed and one twin, we put our suitcases on the twin bed, this room is perfect for two people, ground floor, two windows, service is spot on as well. Room is very clean. Would definitely stay again!

The room was a single,  so it was small. (Not a complaint, just to inform anyone interested.)I was ok with that because I spent most of my time exploring and enjoying the city.  It has an excellent location and friendly staff.  I didn't opt for the breakfast because I was out exploring so I have no opinion about it. I will stay here again in the future.

A floor one bedroom with ensuite bathroom. Very clean and comfortable and has all the necessary features. Very quiet and you can choose breakfast if you like. Very close to Piazza San Marco, Ponte Rialto and the famous Cafe Florian which is an incredible experience for breakfast. Go early 9am to avoid queuing.  Amazing atmosphere and nice restaurants and people

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: A truly wonderful experience, we arrived by private transfer. This is the most expensive option but an experience that cannot be put in words as you arrive via the grand canal to the private garden entrance. The view from our window was to die for, seeing the city come to life each day was truly magical.||Madame Garden is not really a hotel, don’t expect generic service and breakfast buffet. The breakfast is served from the trolleys  brought to your table. Fruits , eggs and delicious pastries each day.||This is probably the best hotel I have stayed in , true memories.||A big thanks to all the team from Helen and Matthew

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Each of the reviews I read are spot-on: this is a fantastic place to stay! The location is absolutely perfect: about a 15-minute walk to the Rialto Bridge, and about 25-minutes to St. Mark's Square. It is so quiet, yet just minutes away from shopping and restaurants. The garden will thrill you! It so beautiful and peaceful. Breakfast (which is amazing) on the patio will get your day started the right way. We had the Iris canal/garden suite. It was so big and comfortable. Plus, we were right next to the canal and spent a lot of time watching the traffic on the canal. The staff, Mara, Alberto, Rippon and Lorenzo were fabulous. They will take care of your every need! We spent four nights there and it was perfect. I would not stay anywhere else in Venice. Thank you for taking such good care of us!

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: This is my second stay at Madama. It is perfection. The decor is beautiful, rooms spacious and well set out, a gorgeous garden to chill in and watch the bustle on the canal. Breakfasts are delicious and extensive, you won't need lunch. What really adds to all this splendour is the staff, everyone is so friendly and helpful, lots of great recommendations on restaurants and places to go, you feel really looked after. Can't wait to go back.

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: Beautiful and idyllic boutique hotel in a quiet location which is also within easy reach of attractions. Lovely Mara, the owner and her team are very attentive. Nothing is too much trouble. The breakfast is amazing as are the apartments, especially the comfy beds!

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: What a wonderful personal oasis in Venice! The suite was amazing, with incredible views, and the service was above and beyond. Well worth it! Exactly the kind of romantic boutique hotel we were looking for. Highly recommended!

shop_address: Cannaregio, Fondamenta S. Felice, 3604, 30121 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text:
My wife and I spent two nights in Madame Garden Retreat and it has been an unforgettable experience! On arrival, Alberto welcomed us and confirmed that we have been upgraded for our stay, which was a great start. Our room was clean, spacious and had great views over the canals. The property itself is tucked away from
the main tourist route, allowing you to enjoy a moment of quiet (and shade) in their garden over a glass of Aperol. During our stay, Alberto gave us some great tips that helped us take the most out of the visit. Overall, it felt like staying in someone’s Venetian house rather than a hotel, which made our time more special. We will most certainly be back!

review_text: Simply a wonderful tranquil garden spot to hide from the August crowds and to saunter the canals. Beautiful breakfast (the scrambled egg was just so yummy and there are more pastries, fruit and yoghurt than you could possibly wish for. Comfy bed, pretty decor, and staff who are very happy to make suggestions, seek reservations and to make your life easy. A fabulous hotel.

A timeless favorite in Venice and one of the most enjoyable museum experiences in the city. Entry was €16 per person, and absolutely worth it. The collection brings together some of the most iconic works of modern art — Pollock, Miró, Magritte, Calder, and of course, Peggy’s own spirit running through the house. The museum is compact but deeply personal, with pieces displayed in what was once her home overlooking the Grand Canal. We started with coffee and a slice of chocolate cake at the café (a bit ordinary, but the view made up for it) before exploring the galleries. Intimate, vibrant, and full of history — it’s easy to see why this is a favorite spot for so many visitors.

A must see when in Venice, totally worth the visit. I also recommend spending the extra €7 (on the steep side, to be fair) for the audioguide since there's an abundance of information regarding the artists and the collection in general (and the collectors themselves, of course). While a small gallery, the switch from garden to rooms to terrace (with exhibits) gives a nice and sophisticated feeling itself. You'll find the chance to see original Kandinsky, Picasso, Dali and Ernest (and many other famous contemporaries). I hope this helps 😊
P.S. while we booked in advance for early morning, there was no difficulty to also purchase tickets there. However, there was much more visitors than we expected, especially this early (first hours of opening).

WORLD CLASS! 👍👍👍👍👍
Here you will find a lot of the best artists of the best, a top place to visit in Venice. 🥰
They have a nice café. ☕🍷🍺
Entrance 15€, discount of you are owner 70 years old. 🙏
Picasso, Miro, Kandinsky and more of those guys.
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

An absolute must when in Venice. The collection is incredible – not overwhelming in size, but perfectly curated so you can really enjoy each piece without feeling rushed. Walking through Peggy Guggenheim’s former home adds such a personal touch, and it feels almost like being invited into her world.

The sculpture garden is a highlight – peaceful, inspiring, and a lovely spot to pause before heading back inside. I loved how accessible the museum feels, even if you’re not an art expert, you can still connect with the works and the stories behind them.

It’s one of those places that stays with you long after you leave.

Beautiful historic complex by the Canale Grande and quite an impressive collection. They also have temporary exhibitions of good quality. Be mindful though that accessibility is only warranted in the sculpture garden. Everywhere else there’s stairs up and down into and in the buildings.

Probably the most visited museum for modern art in Venice. To avoid waiting for an hour in front of the ticket office, buy your ticket on the internet. The art is safe, e.g. Mrs Guggenheim preferred to invest in known artists rather than taking risks. Much of the pleasure of coming here is negatively affected by the affluence of visitors. Plan your visit accordingly if you can.

I enjoyed Peggy Guggenheim.  I am also a fan of contemporary/ Modern Art!  I am fascinated at how creative and ahead of their times the artists that she collected were in the 1930's.   Get the audio version, the back story helps you understand the relationships that Peggy had (or did not have) with the artists.
If you don't like contemporary art, go for the view it is beautiful overlooking the canal or in the garden.  Venezia!!! Venice is eternity itself.❤️

Nice collection of modern artwork from Cubism, surrealism and abstract art and sculptures.  Works of Salvador Dali, Picasso and Joan Moro among others are displayed. Location along the Grand Canal is an ideal location. We spend quite a lot of time there. A nice cafe offers refreshing drinks too.

review_text: What a great B&B this is!||||The apartment is located in a peaceful area of Venice great for exploring with good restaurants nearby. The host Roberto was very welcoming and did everything to make sure we enjoyed our stay. He speaks very good English so any questions he is able to answer. The apartment was clean and spacious and included everything we needed. Highly recommended. ||||Giuseppe and Carline

shop_address: Calle Seconda de la Fava, 1127, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: My husband and I stayed for four nights in Roberto and Marina's apartment. It was in a fantastic location just off via Garibaldi with lots of lovely restaurants, market stalls and bars nearby. It was very easy to arrange with Roberto and Marina and Roberto showed us around and how everything worked. The price was very reasonable and they even include croissants, yoghurt, juice, tea, coffee, jam etc for breakfast. They checked on us by email to make sure we had everything we needed and were perfect hosts. I would definitely stay there again and highly recommend it to couples looking for space and facilities in a lovely area which is very close to the main tourist areas but has a local feel.

shop_address: Calle Seconda de la Fava, 1127, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: I can’t recommend Roberto and Marinas apartment enough! Roberto couldn’t have been more welcoming and lovely when we checked in, he met us outside his home and walked us to the apartment, showed us around and gave us recommendations for dinner! He made us feel very at ease and welcome! The apartment was lovely and well furnished with everything you could need for a short stay (we stayed 4 nights). It was in a perfect location, with shops and restaurants within a five minute walk and the main attractions within a 15 minute walk. Lovely peaceful area, away from all of the hustle and bustle. We hope to stay here again soon! Thank you Roberto and Marina :)

They were great too!!||||The Apartment had more then enough space for the three of us and was equipped with the needed essentials for an enjoyable stay. ||||We would definitely stay here again when we visit next time.

shop_address: Calle Seconda de la Fava, 1127, 30122 Venezia VE, Italy
review_text: I cannot recommend this B&B enough! I was only in contact with and met with Roberto on my short stay. Roberto also provided me with a phone number to contact if I got stuck or had any other questions to which he responded to very quickly.||The location where the B&B is situated is perfect! It's so peaceful and quiet enough to be away from all the busy areas but also has so much going on around it. There are many lovely restaurants, bars and a beautiful park nearby with very convenient water taxi stops close too. St Mark's square is also within a short 10 minutes walking distance too.||Roberto showed us exactly how everything worked within the apartment from the TV to the Air conditioning to the kitchen appliances and coffee maker. He also left the fridge full with refreshments and continental breakfast items. review_text:

The seating is in a nice garden with some tables that even have a nice view of the adjacent canal. The food was decent, but the service was a bit confused.

Would have given 4 stars but they put out this bread on the table that was clearly just plain white store bread that wasn't even tasty. Why not get some nice bread for your customers? There's even a kosher bakery a minute away that sold nice looking breads.
